new_classes.label_en: Other Still Wine in Containers of More Than 2 Litres with High Actual Alcoholic Strength (CN 22042996)
new_classes.label_de: Anderer Stillwein in Behältnissen von mehr als 2 Litern mit hohem tatsächlichem Alkoholgehalt (KN 22042996)
new_classes.definition_en: Still wine of fresh grapes, not bearing a PDO or PGI, in containers of more than 2 litres, with an actual alcoholic strength exceeding the threshold defined in additional note 4 to Chapter 22 (total dry extract criterion applies); classified under CN code 22042996.

coverage_explanation: The proposed axioms capture the key structural discriminators for CN 22042996: (1) still wine (not carbonated), (2) containers of more than 2 litres, (3) absence of PDO/PGI status, and (4) membership in the 'other' residual group (22042922–22042998 range). However, the additional notes 4 and 5 referenced in the legal text introduce quantitative criteria (total dry extract, non-volatile constituents including sugars, glycerol, tannins, tartaric acid, colouring matter, salts) and alcohol-strength thresholds that discriminate this specific code (22042996 = high actual alcoholic strength subcode within the 'other' group of large-container still wines). OWL datatype properties for 'total dry extract' and 'actual alcoholic strength' thresholds are not yet defined in the TBox and cannot be fully formalised without introducing new properties. The disjointness from PDO and PGI large-container classes is modelled via complement restrictions. The specific additional-note thresholds (dry extract composition and alcoholic strength cut-off) remain partially uncovered due to missing datatype properties for dry-extract content.
